epic.description: Goal: Analyze the legacy MSSQL database, design the target PostgreSQL schema, define all API contracts, and document the solution structure. Zero production code in this sprint. Agents: Architect Agent. Duration: 1-2 days. Estimated cost: ~$8-12.

stories.summary: [ARCHITECT] Analyze MSSQL .bak file and extract legacy schema
stories.description: Restore the 4.4MB MSSQL backup. Extract all DDL. Document every table/column/FK/index. Map legacy customers + contact history to new schema. Flag PII fields. Map MSSQL types to PostgreSQL. Outputs: legacy-schema.md, migration-mapping.md

Design full PostgreSQL schema: Projects, Users, UserProjectRoles, Customers, ContactHistory, Notes, CustomerTasks, Opportunities, SyncLogs, RefreshTokens, AuditLogs. Every table has Id(UUID), CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, IsDeleted, ProjectId. Define all enums, indexes, FKs. Outputs: db-schema.md, entity-stubs/

stories.summary: [ARCHITECT] Define API contracts and OpenAPI spec
stories.description: Define all REST endpoints: Auth, SuperAdmin, Customers, ContactHistory, Notes, Tasks, Opportunities, Sync, Dashboard. Specify request/response schemas, required roles, error codes. Define SaaS A and SaaS B inbound payload formats. Define outbound callback payload. Document ApiResponse<T> wrapper. Output: api-contracts.md

stories.summary: [ARCHITECT] Define solution folder structure and scaffold plan
stories.description: Document exact .NET solution folder structure (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API, Tests), frontend structure (React+TS+shadcn), CI/CD workflows, Docker Compose setup. List all required env vars in .env.example. Outputs: project-structure.md, .env.example
